pullRequest.title: feat[lang]: deprecate values as default parameters for interfaces
pullRequest.body:
### What I did

Implement #4393 
Fix #3170

### How I did it

### How to verify it

### Commit message

```
this commit adds the ability to specify `...` as the default value for
parameters in interfaces (both inline and vyi files). it also emits a
deprecation warning when a concrete value is used in its place. there
are two aims behind this change:
1. make it clearer that the default value is not used
2. differentiate from abstract methods, where the default value must be
   obeyed
```

### Description for the changelog

### How I did it

### How to verify it

### Commit message

```
this commit updates the venom parser to use the LALR(1) algorithm,
which is O(n) rather than the O(n^3) earley algorithm.

### How to verify it

### Commit message

```
this commit fixes output bundle construction when there is a semantic
error during compilation. it refactors the `ImportAnalyzer` so we
can get all reachable imports as soon as the import analyzer has run,
without needing to run full semantic analysis.

pullRequest.title: fix[lang]: disallow events from having modules as members
pullRequest.body:
### What I did

Fix #4601

### How I did it

### How to verify it

### Commit message

```
this commit fixes the compiler panicking on events with modules as
member. this is done by moving the struct check up to all user-defined
types. at the moment, `_as_tuple_member` is true for all types except
modules, fulfilling our requirement.

this commit also makes the crash message on `abi_type` not being
implemented clearer. this message should never be seen by users, so that
change is only useful for compiler developers.
```

pullRequest.title: feat[test]: add test cases for duplicate built-in imports
pullRequest.body:
### What I did
Fix #4603

### How I did it

### How to verify it

### Commit message

```
test[lang]: add test cases for builtin duplicate import detection

this commit adds tests for two edge cases around duplicate detection of
builtin imports, as a follow-on to 40375c3ce:

1. importing a builtin module twice (e.g., `import math` / `import math
   as math2`) raises `DuplicateImport`
2. importing a builtin and a local module with the same name (e.g.,
   `import math` and `from . import math`) are correctly treated as
   distinct modules, including for deep paths (`ethereum.ercs.IERC20`)

aliases are added to the imports to make sure the error is not shadowed
by a different one.

also remove unnecessary `contract.vy` from input bundle in existing
`test_reject_duplicate_imports`.
```

### Description for the changelog

### Cute Animal Picture

pullRequest.title: fix[lang]: add missing `HexBytes` code paths
pullRequest.body:
### What I did
- fix: https://github.com/vyperlang/vyper/issues/4608

### How I did it
- added `HexBytes` to the type checks

### Commit Message
```
HexBytes nodes (e.g., x'0102') were missing from several code paths
where Bytes nodes were handled, causing compiler panics.

this commit adds HexBytes handling to:
- `_literal_int()` conversion in _convert.py
- sign extension for signed integers
- `len()` builtin's constant folding

it also removes `Bytes` from the sign extension path in
`_literal_decimal()` since Bytes cannot actually be passed into that
function.
```

pullRequest.title: fix[parser]: generate unique nodes from python AST singletons
pullRequest.body:
### What I did

Fix #4676.

The root cause is that operator nodes are [singletons](https://docs.python.org/3/library/ast.html#ast.AsyncWith) in python.

The existing workaround does not appear to resolve this issue for operator nodes (not sure why) as operator nodes for the same operation have identical metadata (e.g. node_id, col_offset) which is based on the first operator in the contract: https://github.com/vyperlang/vyper/blob/c216787f5e355478733a05fa5f0fce93fa9a7126/vyper/ast/parse.py#L100-L103

For example, if there is a "+" on lines 2, 5 and 10, the metadata for all the "Add" nodes would be that of line 2, even though each "Add" node is an unique object.

The superficial fix for #4676 is to use the parent node (e.g. BinOp) for the exception message. However, the underlying issue that there are duplicate nodes for identical operators would remain. I think we should fix this underlying issue as well.

### How I did it

Add a new `SingletonVisitor` that replaces operator nodes with a deepcopy.

### How to verify it

See the new tests, which fail on `master`.

### Commit message

```
this commit fixes an issue where operator nodes (e.g., `+`) would
be annotated with the same source info, resulting in confusing error
messages for exceptions involving operator nodes. this is due to the
python parser parsing all operator nodes as singletons (see note). this
commit addresses the issue by creating a (shallow) copy of the node
prior to annotating it, so all nodes in the AST are distinct objects.

since the new approach generically handles `python_ast.expr_context`,
a prior special-case fix for the Load/Store AST nodes can be removed.

this commit also fuses the location marking with the
`AnnotatingVisitor`, which improves clarity (and also performance,
since the number of passes is reduced from 2 to 1).

references:
- https://docs.python.org/3.13/library/ast.html#ast.AsyncWith

  > When a string is parsed by `ast.parse()`, operator nodes
  (subclasses of `ast.operator`, `ast.unaryop`, `ast.cmpop`,
  `ast.boolop` and `ast.expr_context`) on the returned tree will be
  singletons. Changes to one will be reflected in all other occurrences
  of the same value (e.g. `ast.Add`).
```

### Description for the changelog

### Cute Animal Picture

![Put a link to a cute animal picture inside the parenthesis-->](https://encrypted-tbn2.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcQcTPunDhUqg6YqK6NWVXxQQyMWifVPfQnlTOknXoQmL8lP9pLmqmB6K9m7H9QL23ljbrhCX9mQZS7aO2kmqQji3IDDCBFOvmG0uQpGxg)

pullRequest.title: fix[lang]: detect duplicate imports via different relative paths
pullRequest.body:
### What I did

Fixed duplicate import detection to catch when the same module is imported via different relative paths (e.g., `from . import x` and `from ..pkg import x`).

Fixes #4770

### How I did it

The duplicate check was using the relative import path as the key, but different relative paths can resolve to the same file. Now it checks both:

1. **Fast check** (before disk I/O): Uses the relative path to catch obvious duplicates like `from . import x` twice
2. **Resolved path check** (after file load): Uses `file.resolved_path` to catch different relative paths that resolve to the same file

Added a `_check_duplicate_import` helper that uses the resolved path as the key.

### How to verify it

Create a test directory with:
```
test_duplicate/
  test.vy
  x.vy
```

`x.vy`:
```vyper
# empty module
```

`test.vy`:
```vyper
from . import x as x1
from ..test_duplicate import x as x2

@external
def foo() -> bool:
    return True
```

Before fix: compiles without error (silently allows duplicate import)
After fix: raises `DuplicateImport: x2 imported more than once!`

### Commit message

```
The duplicate import check used the relative import path as the key, but
different relative paths can resolve to the same file (e.g., `from .
import x` and `from ..playground import x`).

Use resolved path to detect duplicates. The check is performed at the
join point after `_load_import()`, which avoids duplicating the check
across the `.vy`, `.vyi`, and `.json` branches.
```

### Description for CHANGELOG

Fixed a bug where the same module could be imported multiple times using different relative paths.

### Cute Animal Picture

![hammerhead](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/6/6c/Hammerhead_Shark_-_Marko_Dimitrijevic.jpg)

pullRequest.title: fix[ux]: handle function parameters in loop variable error message
pullRequest.body:
when constructing the "Cannot modify loop variable" error, the code assumed `decl_node.target.id` exists. for function parameters, `decl_node` is a `vy_ast.arg` node which has `.arg` instead of `.target.id`, causing a `CompilerPanic`.

apply the same `isinstance(var.decl_node, vy_ast.arg)` pattern already used in `base.py`.

add regression test for the fix.

closes #4797

### What I did

Fixed a compiler panic that occurred when trying to display an error message for modifying a loop variable that is a function parameter.

### How I did it

Added a check for `vy_ast.arg` nodes in `local.py:694`. Function parameters use `.arg` to get the name, while variable declarations use `.target.id`. Applied the same pattern already used in `base.py:279-282`.

### How to verify it

```vyper
@internal
def boo(a: DynArray[uint256, 12] = []):
    for i: uint256 in a:
        a[0] = 1
```

**Before:** `CompilerPanic: unhandled exception 'arg' object has no attribute 'target'`
**After:** `ImmutableViolation: Cannot modify loop variable 'a'`

### Commit message
```
when constructing the "Cannot modify loop variable" error, the code
assumed `decl_node.target.id` exists. for function parameters,
`decl_node` is a `vy_ast.arg` node which has `.arg` instead of
`.target.id`, causing a `CompilerPanic`.

apply the same `isinstance(var.decl_node, vy_ast.arg)` pattern already
used in `base.py`.

add regression test for the fix.
```

### Description for the changelog

Fixed compiler panic when modifying a function parameter used as a loop iterator

### Cute Animal Picture

![tree-kangaroo](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c9/Matschies_tree_kangaroo_Dendrolagus_matschiei_at_Bronx_Zoo_1_cropped.jpg)

pullRequest.title: fix[venom]: handle overlapping reserved memory intervals in allocator
pullRequest.body:
Fix #4800 

## Summary

Fixes a deploy-time OOG regression in Venom when contracts use both immutables (pinned at offset 0) and dynamic string hashing in constructors (e.g., snekmate ERC20 with EIP-712).

The memory allocator could move its pointer **backwards** when processing overlapping reserved intervals. This allocated new buffers inside already-reserved regions, causing:
- Memory aliasing between immutables and dynamic string buffers
- Corruption of string length words when immutables were written
- `keccak256` reading garbage lengths → massive memory expansion → OOG

## Root Cause

The bug was latent in the allocator but exposed by commit `2173a4b94` ("immutables done with alloca"), which introduced:
- Pinned `alloca` for immutables at offset 0
- Copies of that pinned alloca inside internal functions

This created overlapping reserved regions in constructor code paths. When reserved intervals overlap (e.g., immutables at `[0,480)` and a temp buffer at `[160,256)`), the allocator's `if resv_ptr < ptr` branch incorrectly reset the pointer to `resv_end` (256), landing inside the immutables region.

**Fix**: Ignore reserved intervals that end before the current pointer (`if resv_end <= ptr: continue`). This keeps allocation monotonic and treats overlapping reservations as their union.

## Alternatives Considered

1. **Monotonic pointer (chosen)**: Minimal change, correct for all overlapping intervals, no extra bookkeeping.

2. **Coalesce reserved intervals**: Merge overlaps before allocation. More complexity, no practical benefit over monotonicity guard.

3. **Avoid overlaps at source**: Mark immutables volatile or allocate temp buffers in non-overlapping regions. Increases memory usage and doesn't fix the allocator bug for other overlap scenarios.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) and [Codex](https://openai.com/codex)


commit message:
```
The memory allocator could move its pointer backwards when processing
overlapping reserved intervals, causing memory aliasing. This manifested
as deploy-time OOG in contracts using both immutables and dynamic string
hashing in constructors (e.g., snekmate ERC20 with EIP-712).

When reserved intervals overlap (e.g., immutables at [0,480) and a temp
buffer at [160,256)), the old `if resv_ptr < ptr` branch would reset the
pointer to `resv_end` (256), landing inside the already-reserved
immutables region. This caused:
- Memory aliasing between immutables and dynamic string buffers
- Corruption of string length words when immutables were written
- `keccak256` reading garbage lengths -> massive memory expansion -> OOG

The bug was latent but exposed by 418dbda60 ("feat[venom]: remove
`IRAbstractMemLoc`, improve memory allocation") which introduced pinned
`alloca`s at offset 0 with copies in internal functions, creating the
overlapping reservation pattern.

Fix: skip reserved intervals that end before the current pointer (`if
resv_end <= ptr: continue`), keeping allocation monotonic.

Resolves GH issue #4800

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <noreply@openai.com>
```

pullRequest.title: feat[tool]: add storage layout to test exports
fix[venom]: snapshot liveness in `BranchOptimizationPass`
pullRequest.body:
### What I did
Fix a `KeyError` in `BranchOptimizationPass`. The pass uses `LivenessAnalysis` for a heuristic that compares live-variable counts of successor blocks to decide whether to flip `jnz` branches. When the pass inserts a new `iszero` instruction via `add_before`, the instruction list is mutated, and subsequent lookups via `live_vars_at()` can raise `KeyError` since the newly inserted instruction was not present when liveness was originally computed.

Fixes #4920

### How I did it
Snapshot the per-basic-block liveness state at the start of the pass (`heuristic_liveness`) so the heuristic always reads stable pre-mutation data rather than querying the now-stale `LivenessAnalysis`.

### How to verify it
The test in `tests/unit/venom/test_branch_opt_liveness.py` exercises the crash scenario from #4920.

### Commit message

```
BranchOptimizationPass uses LivenessAnalysis for a heuristic that
compares live-variable counts of successor blocks to decide whether to
flip jnz branches. When the pass inserts a new `iszero` instruction via
`add_before`, the instruction list is mutated and subsequent lookups via
`live_vars_at()` can raise KeyError since the newly inserted instruction
was not present when liveness was originally computed.

Snapshot the per-basic-block liveness state at the start of the pass so
the heuristic always reads stable, pre-mutation data rather than
querying the (now stale) LivenessAnalysis.
```

### Description for the changelog

### Cute Animal Picture

![Put a link to a cute animal picture inside the parenthesis-->]()
